name: Echo
lines: Phew... I've confirmed the mic is on, so please stop calling me.
It's depressing to realize I'm the only sane person in this terminal every single day.
Stop wasting time listening to my broadcast and watch your step. Don't get blood on my floors.

friendly.greeting: Well, if it isn't our 'celebrity' Necromancer. I'll broadcast from your perspective as a special favor, so why don't you cause an even bigger scene?
friendly.farewell: Don't die on me. Thanks to you, the ratings... I mean, my boredom is actually fading a bit.

normal.greeting: Attention. Once again, someone is making a scene and wasting the terminal's precious budget. Yes, I'm talking about you.
normal.farewell: Ending broadcast. If you're done, turn off the mic and disappear. I barely have enough time for a smoke break as it is.

hostile.greeting: Look at that Resistance lackey crawling this way. Don't knock on the booth door with those filthy hands; you'll smudge the camera lens.
hostile.farewell: I'll record your screams and use them as a lullaby later. It'll be a masterpiece.

deathLine: This tedious broadcasting... is finally over... Ha...
description: A female announcer holed up in the terminal's broadcast room, spitting out all sorts of venomous remarks. In reality, she might be enjoying this chaos more than anyone.
talk1: Oh my, are you the new Necromancer?
Nice to meet you. Welcome to hell.
As you can see, this dreadful Terminal is bursting at the seams with phantoms again today.
I'm already looking forward to seeing what kind of absurd, ridiculous orders our almighty Reaper will drag us into next.
Man, both of us are just terribly cursed with bad luck.
Landing a job in a complete train wreck of a workplace like this.
But hey, what can you do? We're just grunts who have to do what we're told.
Since we're working together now, I'd prefer if we kept things pleasant between us.
So please, don't go dying on me. Hang in there like a stubborn weed, okay?
talk2: …Wow. I honestly didn't think you'd make it back alive. On top of that, you completely wiped out those disgusting 'Belphegor' flesh heaps?

I thought the Reaper just sent you on a suicide mission, so I was silently praying for your soul. Guess I severely underestimated your abilities. Managing a whole corpse swarm like that? Pretty impressive.
Congratulations, rookie Necromancer. Thanks to you, I've got some juicy breaking news for today's Terminal broadcast. Keep up the good work; I'd love to see more of those spectacular skills.
talk3: Watch your back while you're out there, Necromancer.
I've been getting reports that those pesky Resistance rats are skittering around the corners of the Terminal lately.
Honestly, I couldn't care less whether they're crying for justice or throwing a revolution.
But what if, by some miracle, they actually pull it off and flip the script?
I've been blasting propaganda as the Reaper's puppet all this time, meaning my neck is officially on the chopping line if things go south.
My life and this cozy broadcast chair are literally at stake here.
So if you happen to run into them, don't get all caught up in some self-righteous drama. Just crush them quietly.
Unless you want to see me get fired… or rather, see my head rolling on the floor, that is.
talk4: Ahaha! Judging by the look on your face, the Reaper just chewed you out for something, didn't he?
Let me guess—whining about why you smashed up his precious cargo bay Golem?
Unbelievable. The Golem charges at you to crack your skull open, and he expects you to just stand there and die nicely? Right.
Don't worry, I completely get where you're coming from. It was pure self-defense.
Besides, the Resistance has been messing things up everywhere lately, and the Terminal's control system is completely fried. Nothing works properly anymore.
Anyway, it sucks, but what can you do? That's the life of a powerless grunt. Next time you smash something, just do it quietly so the noise doesn't bleed into my studio.
talk5: …There you are. So old Charon finally got cleaned out by your hands, huh?
I knew he'd cross the line eventually since he broke the Terminal rules, but… actually seeing him go feels a bit strange.
He was a stubborn old fool who never listened, but he was still an old colleague who'd occasionally buy me coffee.
I'm not blaming you. It just goes to show how damn hard it is to stay sane in this godforsaken Terminal.
Sigh… Once I wrap up the broadcast today, I'm going to smoke a heavy cigarette for him. You ruined my mood, so just leave me be.
talk6_1: Oh, wow. I heard you rescued those high-and-mighty VIPs from the Resistance's hands?
The people lounging there are rolling in cash but completely rotten to the core. I usually trash-talk them all the time.
Still, I'm glad you stopped them before they got ransacked. If they actually got kidnapped, my head would've exploded before I could even air the broadcast.
I wonder if management threw a fat bonus your way for saving those precious lives?
Anyway, good job. You basically saved my life this time, so I'll give you a proper thank you.
talk6_2: Oh dear, what's with the long face? Don't tell me you let the Resistance snatch those precious VIPs right from under your nose?
Hey, don't beat yourself up over it. It's not like you let them go on purpose. Those rats are just vicious.
Then again, they messed with the VIPs… so those poor Resistance fools are officially out of time.
I can already feel the shivers just imagining what kind of living hell the furious Reaper is going to show them.
They practically walked into their own graves, so I almost feel bad for them. As for you, go write an apology letter before your own head gets cracked.
talk7_1: Incredible. You absolutely pulverized the main weapon the Resistance was so proud of?
At this rate, the Reaper should be touched enough to hand over some shares of the Terminal to you.
Now that those troublemakers took a fatal blow, our workplace should be peaceful for a while.
…But why the face? You won the battle, so why do you look like someone who's about to go tear the whole world apart?
Well, you're probably just exhausted. Go wipe the blood off your face before you head off to bask in the glorious Reaper's favor.
talk7_2: …Haha. Of all things, this is how that miracle rolls. To think you were a mole for those Resistance bastards all along.
How stupid of me to warn *you* not to get involved with those rats. Talk about a blind spot; I played myself beautifully.
Now that the Reaper is dead and the tables are turned, my life as the propaganda announcer who parroted his words is practically over.
And here I thought we actually became decent friends, sharing cigarettes in this messed-up Terminal… I guess I was the only one who was genuine.
I don't blame you, so just go. Whether you chop off that mighty Reaper's head or not, I'll just be here packing up my studio for the last time.
